Implemented for the requested native validator lifecycle and fresh-genesis path; I did not restart the chain.

Why it matters: validator approval, join, stake update, signer update, exit, and withdrawal now run as native Gold Chain flows using `GILT`, not Ethereum bridge event fields. The old bridge staking event path no longer mutates validators. Fresh genesis now enforces a minimum 4-validator launch model, validator power cap, native approvals, `GILT` escrow, and duplicate-IP as test-only config.

Checks passed:
- `go test ./...` in `bridge/gilt-consensus`
- targeted stake/bridge/app/gilt/topup tests
- `make build`

Next: when you say so, start a brand-new fresh chain again and verify live 4+ validator block production, one-validator restart/catchup, and no bridge-side validator mutation.
